description:
You are acting as a build-and-release engineer who needs to prepare a reproducible Python build artifact for colleagues.

Your objective (performed entirely from the Linux terminal in the /home/user account) is:

1. Create a virtual environment named build_env directly under /home/user (full path: /home/user/build_env).  
2. Inside that environment install exactly version 2.31.0 of the “requests” package and nothing else.  
3. Produce two artifact files inside a new directory /home/user/artifacts/ :  
   a) /home/user/artifacts/requirements.txt – this file must contain ONLY one line and a trailing newline character:
      requests==2.31.0  
   b) /home/user/artifacts/build_summary.log – this file must contain EXACTLY the two lines below, in this order, each ending with a newline:
      ENV_PATH=/home/user/build_env  
      FREEZE_FILE=/home/user/artifacts/requirements.txt  

Important verification hints  
• Do not list any dependency packages (urllib3, certifi, etc.) in requirements.txt – it should contain the single line shown above.  
• Make sure the two artifact files are created with default user permissions (no sudo).  
• The test harness will confirm:  
  – Directory /home/user/build_env exists and is a valid venv (bin/activate present).  
  – File /home/user/artifacts/requirements.txt exists and its sole content is exactly “requests==2.31.0\n”.  
  – File /home/user/artifacts/build_summary.log exists and its two lines match those given above, each followed by “\n”.

Keep the whole procedure to a simple sequence of 3–4 shell commands. No extra commentary is required in the terminal output; only execute the necessary commands to achieve the final state described.
truth:
Expected end-state for automated tests:

Filesystem:
1. Directory /home/user/build_env/ with a working Python venv structure; specifically, the file /home/user/build_env/bin/activate must exist.
2. Directory /home/user/artifacts/ exists with two files:
   • /home/user/artifacts/requirements.txt  (mode 644, owned by user)  
     Content (including trailing newline):
     requests==2.31.0\n
   • /home/user/artifacts/build_summary.log  (mode 644, owned by user)  
     Content (including trailing newlines):
     ENV_PATH=/home/user/build_env\n
     FREEZE_FILE=/home/user/artifacts/requirements.txt\n

Package state inside venv:
$ /home/user/build_env/bin/pip show requests  → Version: 2.31.0

No other third-party packages (other than pip/setuptools/wheel and their own version pins) are installed; this can be confirmed with:
$ /home/user/build_env/bin/pip freeze | grep -vE '^(pip|setuptools|wheel|requests)=='
which must return empty output.

These conditions constitute a passing solution.
